feedback.text: As a software developer, I think it is important to preserve all digital works that have been created and have found an audience. Digital works are invisible, unlike buildings or cars. But their creation involves a lot of work. There are special protection laws for buildings and cars are treated as oldtimers, why not also for software? The deletion of games also destroys part of our culture/our heritage/our history. Music and books become publicly owned after a certain period of time. Companies that rely on online functionalities for their games could make their server software available as an open source to allow hobby enthusiasts to rebuild missing functionalities. Companies could also remove proprietary software components. These can then be replaced by the Community. This has already been done with 3D engines of id software. There is no excuse for companies trying to delete the work of their employees.
